text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Ines Fujin is convinced that her sisters' dream is to
dance—until the day of the Arima Kinen, that is.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Her sisters scold her for deciding their dreams for
them, and insist that what they really want is for
her to keep racing.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
And it's not just Ines's sisters who feel this
way—her coworkers from her part-time jobs are
just as captivated by her running.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
When she runs, Ines is doing more than simply
satisfying her own desire—she's giving countless
people hope.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Realizing that it's okay for her to run as much as
she wants, and to enjoy it too, Ines wells up. It's
not long before her cheeks are glistening with
warm tears.
